InformedConsent:
Welcome to the research study!  

 

We are interested in better understanding the nature of dreams.  You will be presented with information relevant to the self-reporting of dreams and some mental health measures. Please be assured that your responses will be kept completely confidential. This survey is one of a series of five. If you are reading this, you completed our initial survey, and this is regarding the first follow-up study 1 month later. Following participation in this survey, you will be invited to additional follow-up surveys in 1 month, 2 months, and 3 months time – all your responses will be linked so that trends can be tracked over time. You will be paid for each follow-up survey. Following the completion of all surveys, there will be a $1.00 bonus. 

The survey should take 10 minutes to complete. After completing the survey, you will be paid $2.00 for your participation. You will be paid an additional $2.00 for each follow-up survey taken. The final survey with the bonus ($1.00 + $2.00) will pay you $3.00 for a total of $12.00. We do not ask for your name or any other information that might identify you. Although collected data may be made public or used for future research purposes, your identity will always remain confidential.


The research will not benefit you personally. We know of no risks resulting from participating in the study. Your participation is voluntary. You may withdraw at any time, and you may choose not to answer any question, but you must proceed to the final screen of the study in order to receive your completion code which you must submit in order to be paid. In accordance with Prolific policies, we may reject your work if the HIT was not completed correctly or the instructions were not followed. Your participation in this research is voluntary. You have the right to withdraw at any point during the study, for any reason, and without any prejudice. 

If you have any questions you can contact me at pcm38@duke.edu, or my advisor at ws66@duke.edu. If you have questions about your rights as a research subject, contact Duke’s Campus Institutional Review Board at campusirb@duke.edu or at 919-684-3030, please refer to protocol #2024-0062. By clicking the button below, you acknowledge that your participation in the study is voluntary, you are 18 years of age, and that you are aware that you may choose to terminate your participation in the study at any time and for any reason.

DreamMemory:
I had been feeling.. off, in a way. Which I've had times in the past where I had episodes like that, but this was way different. One day, out of nowhere, my fiance informed me that for the last 2 weeks she had put up with me putting her down, arguing with her over everything, avoiding physical touch, avoiding any affection and borderline mentally abusing her and that she had had enough. She said she at first I just seemed upset and began unintentionally isolating myself, which she recognizes as the beginning of one of my episodes.. she understands because she has uncontrollable emotion-driven outbursts at times. Anyways, when she informed me of this I could instantly tell she was hiding behind anger so I wouldnt see how bad I hurt her. I instantly felt so many different emotions, feelings and running thoughts that I had no idea what to say. I truly thought thus was out of the blue because up until that moment I actually believed we were doing great and even getting CLOSER than before. I was so confused because I only remembered 3 of the occasions that she singled out in the conversation we had after the initial confrontation. And of those 3 occasions, I thought that 2 of them had been playful, fun little moments where we teased each other and moved on.

 I'll save the rest, but the entire reason I shared this was because that night I had a clear dream for the first time in over a week. I had dreams in that week but they were choppy, mixed up, bits and pieces remembered, etc. None of them had any kind of point or anything. But that night I had a dream that I'll never forget. I had a very, very vivid dream that I was living with my fiance and our two kids, like a normal day in my life. But I couldn't talk to them, I couldn't touch them to hug them or anything, I couldn't communicate with them in any way but the entire time I was going about my day interacting with them, helping with homework, everything but I could only see it. It was like I made a video of an average day in my life, litterally through my very eyes, and made it into a silent movie. It felt like an eternity... towards the end of my dream,  I saw my son grabbing my finger and pulling me to the door so we could go outside. I watched as I put his shoes on him and everything and we were outside playing.. me, my son and my daughter. My daughter plays soccer, and she kicked a soccer ball and made it into the goal and was so excited cause she never made it from that distance before. I was so proud of her, she had been practicing for weeks (in real life). But then, I couldn't hear what "i" said to her, but whatever "I" said instantly made her smile fade away and she looked away from me and was slowly going to pick uo the soccer ball snd put the goal away. When she glanced back at me i saw a tear in her eye and I got extremely emotional. It felt like somebody had hurt my daughter and i was powerless to protect her. , I was talking out loud and I started out yelling and slowly lowered to like, an exclamation i guess. I said "I am so freaking proud of you biggin (nickname I call her cause she was tiny tiny at birth) and I always will be!". After I said it, I realized that she had started smiling again because she had heard me. It was the first and only time I was able to interact with anyone. I got both of the kids inside and they both fell asleep with their mom. Out of nowhere I watched "me" get up and walk to the hall closet, open it up, shut and lock it behind myself and started pushing a light shelf on wheels (our walk in closet has linoleum floors) to the side until the door begins it was visible. My hand reached out and as soon as I touched the handle, I could instantly hear everything. I heard someone say "I know you're there". Slowly the door opened and it revealed a small room, maybe 5×5 with a small bed. And on that bed, with a short chain on each of his legs that led to the far wall was myself. I mean it was litterally me. Someone had put chains on me, put me in a room alone and took over my body and had been living my life. On the wall above the headboard of the bed were tally marks where the real me had been keeping track of the days. There were 14 black tallys and a 15th one with a space between it and 14. Number 15 had been circled and a small note next to it read "no more. This is the day I get my life back." Then the real me jumped up really quick towards "me" and I was instantly standing in the walk in closet by myself, the shelf wasn't moved. I quickly realized I was in control of everything, could hear everything, all that. I was in control, and I turned to open the door really quickly and when I did I slipped and smacked my head on a very solid table and basically that's when I woke up yelling. I don't know why I was yelling but I felt so much better. I wasn't sad, I wasn't on emotional lock down.. Basically I wasn't I'm my episode anymore. I took a minute to collect myself and I realized that I had a very very strong feeling of regret sympathy towards my fiance, and anger towards myself. All I wanted to do was tell her I was so, so sorry even though I had no real recollection of what I wanted to apologize for. I just knew I finally felt all of the emotions and I wasn't depressed, I was back to normal.

DreamMemory: It had started with me walking around what could only be described as a mansion. Dozens of rooms, and with my cousin I was perusing mountains of family history -- from the most banal, like old schoolwork from siblings and cousins tucked away in old desks that had gone disused for a very long time, to the more interesting, like artifacts from long-dead relatives I'd never met. I had taken a particular interest in a collection of phonograph records going back to the Berliner days from a local St. Louis label. I had tried to play a few of them, but I don't remember what the songs actually were. I then proceeded to walk to bedrooms, including my own, which was a large suite with a grand piano; I played something on that, though again I do not know what. Then we went out to a large patio, screened in, with fireplace, and tiki torches, and copious furniture. For some reason I had to explain to my mother (who had shown up) that it existed. And then I sat down with an old friend I haven't seen in years. We end the conversation, and suddenly I was at the dinner table for an Easter celebration at my grandparents' house. The spread was odd. I don't remember precisely what, but it seemed that everything had contained broccoli for some reason. We say grace and begin to prepare our plates. I say happy easter to my grandfather, who simply smiles. At this point my mother and father become very visibly angry and pull out a letter indicating that I had failed out of a university I never actually attended, though I can't remember which it was. They tell me that they are going to go through the records to find out what I had done. This goes on for what seems like an eternity, at which point I decide to walk out. They shout after me; I leave behind my phone, and start walking toward a shopping center near their home. It is dusk. I continue walking. I see people in their garages, celebrating in an odd way. I continue walking. As I walk down Olive Blvd, dusk turns to night turns to day, and I enter a massive school campus. I realize that I am naked for some reason; I cover myself with a towel, horribly embarrassed. I walk down a hallway full of lockers and gawking students. I enter a courtyard which leads to a football stadium. I exit and go into more hallways. An auditorium. Then a gymnasium. I encounter yet another old friend I haven't seen in ages, yet we don't bother talking. I just follow him; he is going to swim practice in a full olympic-sized swimming pool. It would seem I am about to say something when he jumps into the water. I decide to continue walking. Eventually I enter a crowded main street area of a city with many interconnected buildings. There is some sort of parade going on. I have no clue what I was looking for. I pass restaurants and offices and homes; I enter a museum and leave through the back door into another city street, also bustling, with many buildings. I then enter a couple's home. I am -- for whatever reason -- still naked, but now the towel has disappeared. They are not horrified, but rather sympathetic. "Is this what you were looking for?" they say as they hand me a Roman Missal and Bible, and take me to a church where I confess my sins, attend mass, and promise my reconversion. And then I wake up.

DreamMemory: Yes, for about two weeks I felt like something was wrong with me. Like I had no emotions, I had no drive, I didn't care about anything and I felt like I just.. wasn't myself. My fiance finally confronted me about it and that very night I had a dream I was watching someone live my life through my eyes. Like, I could see everything that was going on but I couldn't hear and I couldn't talk or anything. Like I had to sit there and watch someome else control my body. Towards the end of the dream, I watched as I got up and walked to a closet. Unlocked it, and went in. Then moved a dresser to the side and there was a small door. As soon as I saw my hand touch it I started hearing everything and it sounded like a person basically begging to be let put. I opened the door and sitting there on a very small bed, chained to the far wall in a very small room.. was me. I can't explain it but I had this intense feeling that the person tied to the wall was me and I had to let him out. Well, turned around to walk out and "real me" had gotten free and jumped me from behind and tied me up. Finally, everything went black and when I came to I was standing in front of a mirror still in the closet, alone. I could control my body, and I could feel everything all over again. I instantly ran in the living room and scooped both of my kids and fiance up in a big hug and told them all I love them so much, my fiance then looked at me and said "it's about time you came back. The other "you" was a real a*s". I woke up after that and I felt.. fine. Like I hadn't just spent two weeks feeling off. I felt like I was in perfect shape, and I felt good. The memories of the two weeks were a little hazy and It was very strange but I felt so much better that at the time I didn't care.
